transcript:
What you're watching is a slow drain: dozens of glowing dots around the edges — magenta, cyan, coral, amber — slowly losing their color and light to a growing central mass that swells into that dominant yellow blob by the end.

Here's the part that gets me: in the giver rendering function, there's a comment that says "not death—transformation through spending." The code enforces this literally — it never lets a giver's lightness drop below 24, with the note "the spent still glow—embers, not ash." So even the most depleted dot on screen is still mathematically guaranteed a flicker of warmth. And near the end, when the luminosity of a giver drops below 0.15, its velocity actually gets pulled toward its nearest receiver — the code calls them "witnesses gathering near altars." They're not fading into the background; they're choosing to stay close to what they became.

So the piece isn't really depicting loss — it's depicting where the light goes. Every dimming dot is tethered to a brightness it can now only watch from nearby.

actually gets pulled toward its nearest receiver — the code calls them
